items.reason: Knee osteoarthritis evaluation - consider surgical options
items.clinical_notes: 67-year-old male with progressive left knee pain limiting ambulation. Trialed NSAIDs and PT with partial relief. X-ray shows moderate medial compartment narrowing.
Patient with history of CAD status post stent placement. Needs ongoing cardiology management for secondary prevention.

items.message_text: Dr. Thompson, referring Mr. William Thompson (67M) for left knee OA evaluation. He has progressive medial compartment pain over the past year, limiting his walking distance to 2 blocks. He has failed conservative management with NSAIDs and 6 weeks of PT. X-ray shows moderate medial joint space narrowing with osteophyte formation. Given his functional limitations and CAD history, would appreciate your assessment regarding surgical candidacy, particularly any cardiac clearance considerations.

Thank you, Dr. Wilson. Reviewed the imaging and clinical history for Mr. Thompson. The moderate medial compartment narrowing and his functional limitations make him a reasonable candidate for evaluation. I would like to obtain a weight-bearing MRI to better characterize the cartilage loss and evaluate for meniscal pathology before making a definitive surgical recommendation. Given his cardiac history, we will need cardiology clearance prior to any operative intervention. Will see him at his scheduled appointment on 3/10.
